description: Thai baht (THB). Cash is still useful for markets, taxis and small shops.
ATMs often charge a local fee. Withdraw safely and keep cash split.
Popular exchange option in Bangkok with competitive rates.
Most useful ride-hailing app for cities and airport transfers.
Reliable for water, snacks, SIM top-ups and quick breakfasts.
Thailand Digital Arrival Card should be completed before arrival.
Use eSIM or local SIM. Keep offline maps downloaded.
Thailand commonly uses types A, B, C and O. Bring universal adapter.
Only rent with valid permit, helmet and insurance. Photograph the bike.
Avoid gem/tuk-tuk scams and anyone saying a temple is closed without verification.
Tourist Police 1155, general emergency 191, medical emergency 1669.
Hydrate, use mosquito repellent, and check diving restrictions.
August/September is rainy season in many areas. Keep plans flexible.
